title: BUYER RIGHTS NON-EXISTENT
body: Received a faulty item (costed $130AUD) Seller refused to pay return shipping and asked me to make one and that they would reimburse me. It costs me $50.30AUD out of pocket to return the item. They never paid me that back, and when I asked eBay to step in to get the seller to pay it they blamed me for doing what the seller told me to do and never returned my money. There is a whole paper trail of evidence that I did the right thing, and under my consumer rights the seller is supposed to pay the entire return label if item is faulty. I have been on eBay for 15 years and will happily delete my account and never use it again after this. So disappointed that eBay has their as$es so tight they said they don't have the funds to repay the expense! Hope this business goes under!

title: 14-Year Veteran Delisting Everything: Support Failures & Fee Hikes
body: have been a loyal eBay user since September 2012, maintaining a 100% positive feedback rating with nearly 500 total transactions (over 200 as a seller). However, after over a decade on this platform, I have officially cancelled all my active listings and can no longer recommend eBay to fellow sellers.The core of the issue is a complete breakdown in seller protection and support. Specifically:The Shipping Claim "Black Hole": eBay encourages—and effectively mandates—the use of their shipping labels. However, when FedEx damages a package in transit, I am legally barred from filing a claim directly with FedEx because the account belongs to eBay. When I turn to eBay for help, I am met with extreme difficulty in reaching a human being and a complete lack of urgency in resolving these claims. I am left holding the bag for carrier damage I have no power to contest.Rising Costs vs. Diminishing Returns: While selling fees have steadily increased (most categories now exceeding 13%), the level of service has moved in the opposite direction. It is a poor business proposition to pay premium fees for a platform that offers sub-par, automated-only "support" when logistics issues arise.The Loss of Community: eBay used to feel like a partnership between the platform and its power users. Today, it feels like an automated extraction machine.I am a "Top Rated" style seller who takes pride in my shipments, yet I find it safer to stop selling entirely than to risk another damaged package under eBay’s current support structure.eBay, I don't want to leave, but your current trajectory makes it impossible for a small/medium seller to operate with any peace of mind. I am happy to discuss the specific claim numbers that led to this decision if a human representative is actually reading this.

title: Wrong Seller Level Calculation
body: Wrong Seller Level CalculationOur company has 100% service positive feedback level. We check our selling level monthly. we show we had 6 cancelled items (Stock outs) out of 343 sales from April 2025-March 2026.  That's a stockout level of 0.0175. eBay says we are at 2.51% using 6 stockouts of 241 sales. They claim, we had 100 cancelled orders in that time frame which is Ludacris! So, since we are over expectable 2.0 service level, they charge us an additional 6%. We asked for proof of 100 cancelled sales. Jeff their rep couldn't provide it and eventually hung up on me on the service line.

title: I bought a video card for my pc
body: I bought a video card for my pc. A week went buy almost with nothing sent. I message seller and asked to send item or please issue refund. 3 hours later tracking information popped up showing product was delivered all in a 3 hour time frame. Went from absolutely no tracking info to full tracking info showing delievered within 3 hours of asking for the refund, all of a sudden item shows delivered. Seller shows to be in minnesota but tracking shows point of origin in Dallas which is sus to begin with. I told Ebay on a phone call i recieved nothing despite the magical tracking info which was obviously somehow fake and generated. Also i brought up that this seller was only a 2 week account, and i had my account 17 years so who should they believe? They sided with the scammer, which deleted their account after making the scam sale. I have seen this trend develop over the past few years. They will create another scam account and do it again. So i am out almost $500, with no help from Ebay. This has become a scammers paradise avoid like the plague.

title: eBay Suspended My Account and Charged Me Without Explanation
body: I had a very frustrating experience with eBay.A customer purchased an item from me, but while I was preparing it for shipment, my account was suddenly suspended without any clear explanation. Thankfully, I hadn’t shipped the item yet. The funds paid by my customer were held by eBay.I tried repeatedly to contact eBay support to understand why my account was closed and how to resolve the situation with the order. I was asked whether the item had been shipped, and I honestly explained that it had not. I also had a phone conversation with an agent trying to clarify the reason for the closure, but I was only told my account would not be reinstated and that I could no longer sell. No concrete reason was provided.Since then, I stopped using my account entirely and didn't log in for a long time, only to later accidentally discover unjustified charges added to my account regarding that order, despite the fact that I did not ship the item and never received any payment — all funds remained with eBay. I now have a negative balance that I do not owe.This situation has been very stressful, especially for someone trying to operate responsibly and provide good service to customers.Regardless of whether you are a buyer or a seller, I strongly encourage you to support small businesses whenever possible instead of supporting giants where even an honest seller has no rights.

title: Corruption Company
body: My recent experience with eBay highlighted a serious issue in how seller disputes are handled. Despite notifying eBay in advance that I would be unavailable for a set period, the case involving my account was processed and closed without any opportunity for me to respond.As a new seller, I expected at least a basic level of procedural fairness. Instead, the decision was made unilaterally, without considering the information I provided or allowing me to participate in the process.I’ve retained all correspondence, screenshots and the full case timeline. This isn’t about contesting the outcome — it’s about documenting a pattern that raises legitimate concerns about how seller protections are applied in practice.Anyone reviewing my account should be aware that the dispute outcome reflects a process I was excluded from, not a failure to engage or respond. The record speaks for itself.

title: Money Back Guarantee Policy? What a scam!
body: You trust that a company like Ebay will honor it's policies, but you know about that "Money Back Guarantee Policy" Ebay has? Be aware that they will not honor it. I purchased an above ground pool for $739.92 which was,according to Ebay's policy,included in their "Money Back Guarantee Policy". After the money was taken from my account,immediately after I pressed the purchase button, I received an email saying I would receive a tracking number as soon as my item shipped. The next day I received a tracking number from the seller for my item which was guaranteed to be delivered within 2-4 days from time of purchase. I received a notification from UPS after the third day that my package had been delivered, it was not delivered to me at my address as I was home awaiting the packages arrival. I immediately called UPS to find out where my package had been delivered to and to my surprise my package was delivered to someone else's address and my package had that person's name on the package as well. I found the phone number for that person and gave them a call  hoping to be able to get my package as I believed this was just a horrible mistake. The person did not answer but I received a text with a photo of a box that had the same tracking number I was given by the seller for my package on it and the text said that the package contained 5 alpaca fleece in it, not my above ground pool I had purchased. I immediately contacted the seller and asked them what address they sent my package to and they didn't answer the question but told me they would contact UPS to investigate what happened to my package and would contact me in a few days with the results of that. I contacted Ebay to inform them that the seller took my money and gave me a tracking number that was for someone else's package and apparently it must have been A.I. communicating with me via chat and email because they apparently did not understand the situation and kept giving me the run around. I called Ebay and explained the situation and was told they would investigate it. I explained I had all of the proof to support my claim and show that the seller provided me with someone else's tracking number and they sent me an email to upload that proof. The next email I received from Ebay was to inform me that they had spoken to the seller and the seller informed them that they had sent my item and it had been successfully delivered to my address to me specifically so they were going to close my claim because the seller told them my package had been successfully delivered even though I provided proof that it had not and that the shipping label had someone else's name and address on it and also the tracking number I was provided. They ignored all of the proof I sent them and refused to honor their own policy. This has caused me a lot of trouble and I am in the process with a lawyer to take them to court over this. Talk about shady business. I provided them all of the concrete proof that clearly showed the tracking number I was provided was for someone else's package and that the seller never sent the item they took my money for and yet they accepted the seller just telling them that my item was successfully delivered with no proof of that to back it up. The seller could not have provided them a photo of the shipping label showing that they sent the item to me because I had a photo of the shipping label with tracking number I was provided that had someone else's name and address on it. I had never met that person and did not know who they were, we just happened to live in the same town so Ebay allowed that seller to steal $739.92 from me with no consequences. That seller is still allowed to be on Ebay, so watch out for yoge40.

title: Don’t use eBay
body: They deserve zero starsThey will go out of their way to do absolutely nothing for you. I made the mistake of checking out as a guest, and apparently that’s all it takes for your order to become nearly impossible to manage. Need a return? Good luck, guest orders can’t even be properly retrieved and purchases made as a guest can’t even be reviewed. I tried to leave a negative review for the defective item I received — not allowed.I started a return on March 6th through live chat. The seller was given until March 12th to provide a return label then eBay would escalate the matter. The return label never came. I did manage to finally get a message through to the seller on the 12th only to be told it’s on eBay to fix. I requested a callback from eBay on the 13th, and their idea of escalating the matter was to award me the right to return my purchase, saying they’ll send me a return label which never came. I was already guaranteed a return/refund upon purchase so what did they really do to escalate this. They acted like they were helping while accomplishing absolutely nothing.Then eBay changed their contact system, deactivated the number they called from, and buried access to real support behind an AI chat that feels designed to wear you down until you give up.Now it’s  March 24th — after fighting this all month, I finally get  the ai chat to get me a callback with a live agent to escalate it to the next step only for them to say that they “can’t” issue a refund yet because they have to follow the same broken process that already failed. Their big solution? Send the return label they should’ve sent weeks ago… they don’t escalate past that step apparently and now I get told I have only a couple of days to ship the item back as if I’m the 1 at fault for not returning the item yetSo after weeks of delays, dead ends, and zero accountability, I’m the one scrambling to fix their mess on their timeline trying to fit a trip to the ups store in my already busy scheduleThis isn’t customer service — it’s a masterclass in stalling, deflecting, and wasting people’s time.I’ll be posting this review anywhere that eBay can be reviewed

title: Not Seller Friendly Platform
body: Used to sell in eBay in early 2010s without problems but now this new updated eBay is nothing but a scam haven site for syndicate scammers. Let me explain: I had an auction for a high valued item, I set the listing for no PO box and no international bidders, this was for US only and I received numerous messages to sell outside of eBay via WhatsApp Instagram etc - I declined all. So as the auction nearing to a close, valid US Zip code bidder was about to win the bid, a last minute bot bid came in from "Lithuania". That person then messaged me and said they didn't want to pay for international taxes and fee -(this listing was for no international anyways) so I canceled his. I sent in a second chance offer for the original winning US bidder and eBay automatically relists the entire auction. Another person placed a buy now purchase but his address was a PO Box. His address was impersonating as a residential. Verified through Google maps. What syndicates do is they do that "Item not received"switch thing. Even if you sent a signature confirmation to a PO box, they usually have a freight forwarder at that box, employee signs for it and your package is gone as the buyer will claim he received nothing and file chargeback to cancel his payment. It's a known tactic. Armed with this knowledge and looking at his address impersonating a residential, I canceled that order. Now the actual second chance person made the purchase and his address was a Valid US Address. Needless to say shipping went fine and registered mail he receive it safely in great condition but eBay did not release the funds. In a phone conversation they said a few days after he received the items my funds to be released. Ebay did not honor that claim and now keeping my funds for over a month was their reply after I called back. I would have been okay with two week wait frame but one month is an interest bearing procedure on their end to stall and tie up your funds. They hold your funds to get whatever interest rate off from on the side. This is what the truth of it is. They will not release your funds. As a seller this is not worth it. Oh and the item price sold total was $2100. Ebay took out $400 and are holding that net proceed $1700 for over a month. Even with the buyer leaving a positive feedback and valid signature received. As a seller you will pay out of your own pockets first, do not assume you will get your funds from eBay in any immediate time soon. Todays eBay is not a friendly seller platform. Do not use.

title: ​Warning: Protected Fraud is Rampant on eBay
body: ​I am writing this to warn other buyers: eBay’s "Money Back Guarantee" is failing users against sophisticated "Zip Code Scams."​I recently purchased a high-value item for over $490. The seller provided a tracking number that showed as "Delivered" to my zip code (Waunakee, WI), but the item was never actually sent to my address. Despite providing a manager’s note from the carrier (UPS) proving the package was addressed to a completely different location, eBay's automated system ruled in favor of the fraudulent seller twice.​It is incredibly easy to be defrauded on this app because their dispute system relies on basic tracking delivery status rather than actual delivery verification. I had to go to my bank to file a formal fraud claim just to get my money back because eBay's "protection" was non-existent.​If you are buying anything , be aware that if a seller is smart enough to spoof a tracking number to your zip code, eBay will likely leave you hanging. Use a credit card with strong fraud protection, because you cannot rely on this platform to do the right thing..Ebay will not protect you. If you have an issue with anything. Call your Credit card immediately

title: ebay has gone down the tubes
body: I’ve been buying and selling on eBay for years, and at this point I’m convinced the platform no longer cares about the people who actually use it. The fees are ridiculous, the policies almost always favor the buyer (even when the buyer is clearly in the wrong), and getting a real human in customer service is nearly impossible. You can follow every rule, ship fast, package well, describe your item perfectly—and still have money pulled out of your account because someone changed their mind or didn’t read the description.Between the final value fees, promoted listing fees, payment processing fees, and now fees on shipping and tax, eBay takes a huge cut while doing less and less to support sellers. It feels like they’ve built a system where the seller takes all the risk and eBay takes a guaranteed commission no matter what happens.What’s most frustrating is that eBay used to be great. It used to feel like a marketplace. Now it feels like a corporation squeezing both buyers and sellers from every direction. At this point, “eBay fees” are starting to feel more like a penalty than a service.I’ll still use it because that’s where the buyers are—but let’s not pretend eBay is on the seller’s side anymore. They’re on eBay’s side. Period.

title: eBay says my packages are guaranteed

title: Can't Return What You Won't Let Me Ship
body: I am writing to file a formal complaint against eBay, Inc. regarding a purchase I made through their platform in which I was denied a fair resolution despite following all required procedures.I purchased an item from a seller on eBay, and upon receiving it, I discovered that the item did not match the images or description listed in the original posting. This constitutes a clear violation of eBay's own seller policies regarding accurate item representation.I promptly contacted the seller to address the issue. The seller informed me that eBay handles the return process and that I would receive a return shipping label once I formally requested a return through eBay's system. I followed the return process exactly as instructed.Despite initiating the return request, I never received a return shipping label. I made multiple attempts to resolve this issue by reaching out to both the seller and eBay's customer support team. Neither party provided the return label necessary for me to ship the item back.Despite my documented efforts to resolve this matter, eBay closed my case, stating that I failed to return the item within the required timeframe. This outcome is unjust, as I was unable to return the item solely because neither eBay nor the seller provided me with the return shipping label needed to do so.I believe eBay failed in its responsibility to facilitate a fair return process and then penalized me for a situation that was entirely outside of my control.

title: My husband order snow blower and with…

title: eBay - Probably Worst Overall Consumer Experience I've Ever Had
body: I am absolutely floored by my recent experience with eBay. I know that this review will mean nothing to them, I'm almost leaving it for therapeutic reasons. Because they are the ONLY player in their space, they can do whatever they want, and not worry about the people they are impacting at all.I have been an eBay customer since 2002 if I remember right. Have been selling with a super positive selling profile for several years. Sometimes I would go on a stint where I would sell quite a bit, other times I might not sell at all.Early in 2026, I sold several items. The value of what I sold ranged from trading cards at or around $5 to laptops around $450. USPS was an absolute disaster during this time. They lost several packages, I want to say around 10 of them. The total was north of $600. The buyer requested a refund. Then eBay easily confirmed the item wasn't delivered through tracking, then immediately awarded the refund. I tried talking to someone. It was extremely painful to get a human on the phone. Eventually I did. It was useless. eBay takes no accountability in the process whatsoever. There are no nuances to their processes or policies. It is all on the Seller. They say it's cut and dry, package wasn't delivered, you are liable. Take the money out of your account and give it back to the buyer.I am sure there will be people that say, it's the policy, this is on you. No, it's completely ridiculous policy. They sell a store subscription, that is supposed to award you seller protection and you can bet they market that in a way that feels like real protection, and it is absolutely not. They get sellers to buy this protection and I'm sure they make millions just off of that each year, meanwhile they prey on all of them. Because when it's time to show up and actually protect, not only do they barely even listen to you, but they make it impossible to even get ahold of someone. I'm trying to find some folks that can build a class action suit on this. I'm tired of the lack of accountability that these ridiculous massive businesses have. I may not get anywhere with it, but I'm willing to talk to anyone that will listen, and I know for certain that there are a ton of other people that have had a very similar experience. There are some things that need to be published front and center for anyone making the decision to sell on eBay. I promise you if they were, there would be a ton of people making the decision to go elsewhere. I know I would have.

title: I had a good experience…
body: Order No: 27-14326-82307 Nakajima Electronic Typewriter.I used EBay for the second time in my life last week and just checked out as a guest, hence the missing star in my review. Because I had checked out as a guest I was unable to contact the seller or make an EBay account with my email address. Royal Mail were the carriers. I got emailed last Saturday to say my package was coming between 8am to 11am, it  didnt and I waited until 9.30pm before giving up. Monday I received an email at 1pm saying it would be delivered by 5pm… At 7.33pm I received an email saying Royal Mail had tried to deliver my parcel at 7.23pm but no one was home? I was home, no one was seen on my ring doorbell, the dog didn’t make a sound and no card was put through the door. Their email says they will try again the following day, for the third day I waited… nothing.Because I hadn’t made an account and couldn’t contact the seller because of this I had to get hold of EBay to speak to a human. Whilst not the most straightforward I got there relatively painlessly. Both times I spoke to EBay they listened and confirmed my call via email and I was given a case number. They also put me in direct contact with the seller which was relieving to be able to speak to them direct. The seller ‘network-refurbisher’ Chris was fantastic, I explained everything. Wednesday I received another email saying it was going to be delivered and it arrived! I was worried about any damage to the package due to the rigmarole of getting it delivered but I needn’t of worried it was packed so well. I’m only posting this review because things could of gone really bad and Ive read the bad reviews but my experience was a positive one, Royal Mail, on the other hand, I wouldn’t give 1 star.

title: Counterfeit Cat flea treatment
body: I've bought two items from eBay. Firstly compatible toothbrush heads, which proved to be so hard, my dentist raised concern that my gums had receded. The second item was Frontline plus flea treatment. As l had problems accessing the packaging l wrote & conplained to Frontline plus. After an exchange of emails & photos of the product l was told my purchase was counterfeit. No idea what l was putting on my cat! title: Legitimate US Business Account Suspended on Day 1 With No Explanation — MC999
body: Title: Legitimate US Business Account Suspended on Day 1 With No Explanation — MC999―――――――――――――――――――――――I am the owner of a legally registered US business. I created my first and only eBay seller account to launch my e-commerce operations. Within hours of listing my very first product — without any prior warnings, policy violations, or suspicious activity — my account was suspended under MC999.I have:✅ A legitimate, registered US company✅ No history of any eBay policy violations✅ Only ONE eBay account, ever✅ Never sold counterfeit or prohibited items✅ A verified payment method on fileDespite multiple attempts to contact eBay through their official "Help & Contact" channel, I have received only automated responses with no real human review of my case. No one has explained what specific policy I supposedly violated. No one has given me a fair opportunity to verify my identity or business credentials.eBay claims to support small businesses and entrepreneurs — yet their automated systems shut down legitimate new sellers before they even make a single sale, with zero transparency and zero accountability.I am not asking for special treatment. I am asking for a fair, human review of my account. I have all my business documents ready and I am fully prepared to complete any verification process required.If you are a new seller considering eBay — be warned. Their trust & safety systems are broken and their customer support is virtually unreachable for new accounts.I hope eBay takes this seriously and reaches out to resolve this matter promptly.

title: Have been waiting 4 weeks for a refund…
body: Have been waiting 4 weeks for a refund for parcel which was sent to wrong address and parcel was returned by carrier. I was told I would have a partial refund in 2 to 3 weeks. I have e mailed them and left messages and no one has got back to me. Will never order from them again.

title: EBay you have a special place in hell
body: I’m honestly still trying to process what actually happened with this whole situation, because it’s left me more frustrated then anything else. I sold an inherited item—something that already had personal value attached to it—so it wasn’t just some random thing I could easily replace or not care about. I made sure it was packed properly and sent in good condition, exactly as described, no hidden issues or anything like that.The buyer recieved the bundle and at first there wasn’t much said, which I took as everything being fine. But then suddenly, I get told there’s a problem and they want to return it. What I got back though was not what I originally sent, not even close. Parts of the bundle were clearly missing, like they had just been taken out, and what was left looked like it had been handled carelessly or even damaged on purpose. It’s hard to explain but you can tell when something just isn’t right anymore.The condition it was returned in was honestly upsetting. Items were either broken, scratched, or just not in the same state I had carefully sent them in. It feels like I’ve been completely taken advantage of here, and there’s no real accountability from the buyers side either, which makes it even worse.Overall this has been a really dissapointing experience, especially considering the item meant something to begin with. I wouldn’t feel comfortable going through something like this again, because once something like that is sent back ruined and incomplete, there’s not really anything you can do to fix it.

title: Technically ZERO stars. Disgraceful "Customer Support"
body: Technically ZERO stars, because after this experience even one star feels generous. Specifically decided to make this purchase outside of Amazon, but clearly eBay is far worse. title: I was double charged for Ebay selling…

I offered him the amount that USPS would refund to me for the damaged shipment, but he declined.  Neither Ebay nor the buyer suffered any losses only me as the seller.  Contacted Ebay several times and their only response was this dispute has been closed.  Have since closed my account, removed all products for sale, as I did not want them to continue to charge my back account for unauthorized amounts..

title: Would love to give NO STARS !
body: Would love to give NO STARS if was an option. I ordered two hair products from EBay-as a guest- due to not having account. title: A very very disappointing experience…
body: I had a very disappointing experience with eBay’s buyer protection.I purchased a pair of shoes that were never delivered. The tracking only shows “attempted delivery”, with no proof of actual delivery, no courier identified, and no delivery notice ever left at my address.Despite this, eBay closed the case in favor of the seller and refused a refund.There is:No proof of delivery (no signature, no photo, no confirmation)- No identified courier for the final delivery.- No evidence the item ever reached my address.An “attempted delivery” is not proof that the buyer received the item.I contacted both the seller and eBay multiple times, but the response was simply that the case is closed, without addressing the lack of delivery evidence.This raises serious concerns about how buyer protection is actually applied.I will now proceed through my payment provider, but I expected significantly better handling of such a clear case.Buyers should be aware that even without confirmed delivery, claims may still be denied.

title: Microsoft Office activation codes
body: Microsoft Office - activation codes not valid.  I purchased 2 licenses for MS Office 2021, on Ebay from direct*sales*store.   The licenses arrived... When I went to go and install them, I received a message stating that the activation codes were invalid.  I reached out to the seller 5 separate times, through Ebay, over a 4 week period.  No response.  It's now been 6 weeks, and I still have not heard back.  Even if it's after their warranty period, the activation codes don't expire, and if they are invalid, they were either re-sold, or never valid.   It was $50 purchase. title: Very frustrating experience with eBay…
body: Very frustrating experience with eBay Shipping.A package worth $300 is marked as delivered in eBay’s tracking system, but it has not been delivered. When checking the official Asendia tracking, which contains more detailed information, it clearly shows that the package has not been delivered.Tracking link: a1.asendiausa.com trackingnumber=ESUS302210867  Order number: 19-14016-71774The biggest issue is that there is no way to contact anyone at eBay Shipping to report the problem. title: Ebay has become a very corrupt organization.
body: Ebay has become a very corrupt organization, where Ebay allows frauds by sellers  to go unnoticed  as long as it makes Ebay money. My card was used without my consent to I order a bluetooth speaker from  Ebay last week, and it was paid right away. Right after it was paid, however, I received an email from Ebay that "the item had been removed from the Ebay site, and then the seller was not a registered Ebay seller. If I had already paid, well, not to worry, your item will arrive." I contacted Ebay and the seller to cancel, for this transaction appeared to be fraudulent to no avail. You would think that Ebay would take the initiative to not only apologize  to me for having removed the seller and removed the item from its site but also issue me a refund right away. No, I am left on my own. I have not received a refund. I have tried to contact Ebay, and the organization is set up to make sure that you cannot talk to a live person or write to it. Please don't buy from Ebay, it is a big scam. I will be doing my shopping at Amazon.

title: UK buyer here.
body: UK buyer here.I bought a second hand inexpensive necklace(around 14 dollars) on ebay from a USA seller. Started  receiving emails from DPD demanding passport ID upload !!!!! They were citing anti-money laundering laws for a second-hand cheap necklace which is pure  BS  and  a bare- faced lie. No other delivery company demands this from a UK buyer for delivery to a fixed address. You only need to show ID if you are collecting from a depot . They just want your personal info .The item ended up being sent back to the seller as I would not comply. This is outrageous.  I am furious . I did not choose this rubbish delivery company. As if I would hand in my ID to a dodgy American company who will either sell my info or get hacked and leave me open to fraud with no admission of liability. I have stopped buying on ebay from American sellers as long as they use DPD for delivery. Don't trust these companies with your info people. You will end up regretting it. God knows whose hands your info ends up in. Let's hope ebay takes note and stops using DPD.

title: eBay: A Nightmare Customer Experience
body: Let me make this perfectly clear: my experience with eBay was an absolute disaster and has left me infuriated beyond belief. I spent a lot of money on an item and it was NEVER DELIVERED. After jumping through endless hoops and wasting hours on the phone, FedEx finally admitted that the tracking number the seller provided didn’t match my address—instead, my package was delivered to some random stranger in my city. This is already outrageous, but it gets even worse.eBay’s so-called “customer service” and "money back guarantee" are complete jokes. Their response? Since FedEx’s system claims the package was delivered somewhere in my city, they washed their hands of the whole thing and flat-out refuse to issue a refund! According to them, the sale is “complete” and the item is considered delivered TO ME—even though I never received a thing... Are you kidding me?!This is the most absurd, customer-unfriendly policy imaginable. So apparently, if your package is dumped at any random address in your city, you’re just out of luck. No package, no help, no refund—just endless excuses and finger-pointing. eBay and their sellers either have zero clue how to support their customers or are actively scamming people. Either way, it’s a disgrace.I’m warning everyone I know: DO NOT trust eBay with your money. Their policies are a scam, and their “support” is non-existent. Save yourself the frustration and shop literally anywhere else (TEMU is so much better!). After this experience, I will make sure all my family, friends, and colleagues avoid this platform like the plague. You’ve been warned—AVOID EBAY AT ALL COSTS!eBay is unreliable, unhelpful, and completely uninterested in protecting their customers. Don’t fall for it—take your business somewhere you’ll actually be respected as a customer.You’ve been warned!!!

title: Stay Away from Anything eBay
body: I wish we had the ability to give 0 stars. We bought a Trumpet from a seller that was completely misrepresented. It was in terrible condition and did not even work. We were told we had 90 days to return and then eBay came in and said no, you should have returned within 30 days. We tried to communicate with the seller, but eBay will not allow you to send more than 3 messages within a couple days. What company limits the communication with the seller. That is crazy and shady. I would never recommend eBay, clearly all of the negative reviews are accurate.

title: Speechless.
body: The downfall of Ebay should indeed be studied for generations upon generations. What was once a beloved site, where anybody can get what they want, need or desire, has is now up for question due to it's reputation of missing packages, revolting customer service, scams and much more. My personal experience for over a decade plus has been mixed. No matter what the issue was, I could never rely on customer support to get it right. Even with this year, when a package of mine was literally 10 days plus late/with barely any communication from the seller/Ebay customer support themselves. It is no wonder why a lot of people are looking elsewhere, just to get their daily needs and wants. However, their digital offerings are mostly guaranteed with no hassle, but every other listing on the site is a gamble/it is very imperative that you talk with the seller first BEFORE hitting the buy button. if Ebay doesn'tfigure something out quick, they will possibly be bought about by one of their competitors. Ebay won't be able to blame A.I or the sellers when that happens. They will simply have to blame themselves. As I am writing you this review, my issue still isn't resolved/a partial or full refund, still has not been offered either. I expect this from Ebay. A once great online marketplace, now lost in it's own vision with it's high shipping prices and unreliable tracking software.

title: Bad Experience With EBay Merchant
body: I have recently had a bad experience with eBay when l decided to sell on their website few months ago. I sold an item to a customer. title: eBay wait up!
body: eBay, wake up!I've been a professional seller on eBay since 2005 with a 99.7% positive feedback rating. I'd like to share my experience with other members of this e-commerce platform. About ten years ago, eBay was very competitive with reasonable transaction fees of around 5 to 6%.Today, we have to pay for our store subscription, plus transaction fees ranging from 13% to 22.5%. To get better visibility, you have to add marketing and advertising fees that can reach 20%, not to mention another oddity: a commission on shipping costs of 13% to 22.5%. You can do the math. The Nasdaq shareholders have nothing to worry about. Selfish as overgrown babies, now millionaires, they'll invent some great success stories for us. How incredibly intelligent they are, working harder than everyone else...Furthermore, eBay opens its sales portal to unsafe and high-risk countries. Statistically, the seller has no way of controlling the delivery of packages despite tracking. Unfortunately, in the event of a dispute for non-receipt, eBay does not protect the professional seller and systematically refunds the fraudulent customer.If you contact French customer service by phone, the receptionists are unable to process requests. Professional sellers are forced to communicate by email, given the language barrier and the centralization of requests in certain low-cost countries. Therefore, we are often confronted with amateurs who are neither familiar with French nor European legislation and hand out penalties indiscriminately.Finally, if, unfortunately, you receive insufficient performance due to unresolved disputes, then you will be in serious trouble. Without warning, these amateurish eBay apprentices will reduce your international traffic. This lack of clarity could lead to losses of 60 to 75% of revenue. These same novices will even remove international visibility on ebay.com. In some countries, products aren't listed, resulting in a 100% drop in sales.How can performance be improved if the algorithm prevents sellers from working? What an absurdity and inconsistency! It's a mistake to give the sword to the one who could become your executioner. So anything goes, and professional sellers aren't entitled to compensation.With considerable hesitation, I think it's time we updated our e-commerce site, and reviews will be handled by Trustpilot or Google, etc.

title: OMG.
body: OMG.How many times do I need to state my number has changed?And yet they still refuse to update it.And now my account will be closed as I haven't accessed it in a while.Of course I haven't as I can't and you don't help me me to do so.Morons.Vinted from now on.

title: Seller Used Conterfeit Postage - Zero Help from Ebay
body: There is absolutely no way to contact ebay especially about bad sellers. I had to file a dispute with my credit card thanks to ebay's ZERO customer service. The seller sent out my package with COUNTERFEIT POSTAGE so now my item is property of USPS and I will not get it. The agent I spoke with at my credit card company said ebay was one the worst places with fraudulent sellers. I agree because this is not my first issue with ebay. Sadly, I have had many more before.UPDATE: Ebay removed my review on this seller: wacplorhy_31.This is why you see so many good reviews on ebay. Ebay does not want you to see such fraudulent activity. With bad reviews you only see reviews like "item arrived broken" so they can place blame on USPS. I am no fan of USPS either.

title: Selling is not user friendly at all
body: Selling on eBay is ridiculously complicated. I seemed to have different addresses saved in different places rather than just one address option. Finding the right place to update them was a challenge. When I finally managed to do this so I could post the item my funds were then put on hold because I had to update my 'payouts' bank details. Again there were several different places where bank details are stored and this one happened to be an old bank account. I don't really understand why you can't just add one bank account that's used for everything and one address that's used for everything. When this is updated it should be updated for everything too. I have been using eBay for purchases with my updated address and bank details but unfortunately only my buying address/bank details got updated.  Would not recommend using eBay to anyone - needs to be much user friendly.

title: eBay supports buyer fraud and makes commission on it
body: Selling on eBay means fees stacked on top of fees. Because the platform charges commissions on the full total—including shipping and tax—it’s surprisingly easy for fees to approach or even exceed the value of the item. When fraudulent or frivolous returns are accepted automatically, sellers also pay return shipping and still get hit with final value fees. Sometimes it feels like you’re paying just to give things away.

title: eBay steals from sellers by charging fees on top of fraud
body: In addition to exorbitant fees that apply on top of shipping and tax which means it's very easy to fees to exceed item price (so you're literally paying to give away things), eBay regularly robs sellers blind by accepting fraudulent returns on the seller's behalf and charging shipping and final value fees for the "service" of abusing sellers. Since the protections are far more negative than positive, you are far better off just paying 2-3% credit card processing fees and dealing with customers directly as eBay will only find ways to rob you.

title: Exemplary and outstanding customer support
body: I'm shocked to see so many negative reviews but of course I can only speak for myself. I've been an eBay member since 2012 and in all honesty I cannot recall a single time the customer support hasn't been professional and tried to help. Last time- and this agent (Mr. Khalid) really set the bar, I reached out to them regarding disappointment with slow shipping with Asendia and potentially lost items. The agent started off by thanking me for being a long time member, then waisted no time to immediately resolve the issue. No lengthy process, no excuses or passing the responsibility to me, but straight to the point and fixed it in a minute. I've rarely been treated this well in my 30 years of collecting in general. Thank you eBay and thank you Khalid for being so professional. This is why I love eBay.

title: Criminal Negligence: eBay Rewards Scammers While Stealing $6,000 from Verified Sellers
body: Do not trust eBay with high-value items. I am currently out over $6,000 because eBay has intentionally bypassed its own "Seller Protection" to facilitate a blatant theft. Despite providing valid tracking numbers—which are clearly visible on the order page and confirmed as DELIVERED—eBay manually overrode the facts to refund the buyer.The most cowardly part of their scam? The "Ghosting" Tactic:I have repeatedly engaged with their customer service, providing undeniable screenshots and PDF proof of delivery. The moment I present the evidence that proves the buyer is lying, eBay agents abruptly end the chat and disappear. They literally run away from the truth to keep the scam running and avoid their financial responsibility.eBay is a Corporate Accomplice to Fraud:Proof is Irrelevant: Valid tracking means nothing to them. They find any pathetic excuse to ignore it.Systemic Harassment: They refuse to close resolved cases and allow "zombie cases" to be reopened on the same transaction to force more refunds.Scripted Negligence: You will never get a human response—only canned scripts designed to protect the scammer and drain your bank account.eBay is no longer a marketplace; it is a safe haven for high-value criminals, and the company is taking a cut of the loot through fees while leaving sellers to rot. I am preparing a formal legal filing and a police report for the $6,000 theft they facilitated. SELLERS: GET OFF THIS PLATFORM BEFORE THEY GIVE YOUR MERCHANDISE AWAY FOR FREE.

title: Apparently eBay has been “trying to reach me”
body: I received an email from eBay informing me that my use of the platform has been restricted because I supposedly failed to submit identity or banking documentation.The email confidently states that eBay has been “trying to reach me.” Fascinating, because this is the first communication I’ve received about it. No earlier emails, no account notifications -  just a sudden message implying I ignored multiple requests.To make it even better, the email ends with the classic “please don’t reply to this message.” So the platform claims it’s been trying to contact me, then immediately closes the only communication channel in the message.If documentation is required, it would probably help if the request actually reaches the user first, brainiacs.

title: Pure garbage
body: After over 14 years of using this platform, I am officially done. eBay has transitioned from a marketplace to a sanctuary for false advertisers. They actively profit from deceptive listings while offering zero meaningful protection when the item inevitably arrives misrepresented. Their 'Money Back Guarantee' is a hollow marketing gimmick; in reality, they bury you in automated bot responses and red tape until you give up. By refusing to police blatant false advertising, eBay is no longer a tool for commerce—it is a liability for consumers. Use a specialized competitor or go local; do not give this declining platform your financial data.

title: Permanently banned for no reason immediately after signing up
body: I write as a Maine resident and cybersecurity professional to formally contest eBay’s permanent, unexplained ban of my account—issued without notice, justification, or appeal—after I simply signed up and listed two legitimate items. I was initially restricted upon registration, then reinstated after identity verification. Shortly after listing two items, I encountered repeated site errors when attempting to enter bank details—a technical failure on eBay’s end. Moments later, my account was permanently banned. No policy violation was cited. No evidence was provided. Three separate representatives confirmed: the ban is “permanent and irreversible,” yet refused to disclose why. eBay customer service labeled me a “threat to the community” without explanation. This is not risk prevention—it is algorithmic discrimination. To brand someone a threat while offering zero transparency devalues the term and denies due fairness.The only plausible trigger? My use of a VPN and alias email—standard, legal tools in my field for protecting personal identifiable information (PII). Millions use VPNs for security. Banning users for doing so penalizes privacy-conscious individuals and equates lawful behavior with fraud and criminilizes our human right to rpivacy. If eBay prohibits these tools, it must disclose that policy. It does not. Maine’s Consumer Data Privacy Act (LD 1822) takes effect July 1, 2026.  It grants residents the right to:Access, correct, and delete their personal dataOpt out of data sales and profilingBe free from retaliation for exercising privacy rights eBay collects my name, email, phone, and address. Yet it denies me membership while retaining my data—unlawful under Maine law if retention serves no purpose. I formally request the permanent deletion of all my PII from eBay’s systems. You cannot ban me and hold my data hostage.This case reflects a broader pattern: users banned without cause, explanation, or recourse. While eBay is a private platform, calling someone a “threat” without evidence crosses into deceptive practice—especially when privacy tools are the unseen trigger. I demand:A clear, written explanation for my banConfirmation that all my personal data has been permanently deletedInvestigation into whether eBay’s automated systems unlawfully penalize privacy-protective behaviors This is not just about access—it’s about accountability, transparency, and the right to due fairness in digital commerce.
